reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe. It downloads from an official source with pinned checksum, contains standard AppImage extraction/installation logic, and only performs targeted patching of application files without dangerous patterns like obfuscated code or suspicious network calls.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped auditing the AppImage binary (Beeper-*.AppImage), leaving the actual application code unverified
No review of extracted Electron files during build (e.g., patched .mjs files) which execute at runtime
false_negatives: Potential risks in unverified AppImage binary (e.g., bundled native modules, compromised upstream build)
Runtime behavior of patched 'registerLinuxConfig' function not validated
re_audit_focus: Beeper-*.AppImage contents (via extraction and file review)
learnings: Improve binary handling: Extract AppImages/ZIPs and audit contents when feasible
Prioritize files modified during build (e.g., patched JS files) over raw binaries
Enhance detection of Electron-specific risks (native modules, preload scripts)
Add explicit checks for common binary package risks: license compliance, supply-chain verification
